name: Riboswitch Regulation (TPP)
description: TPP riboswitch: an aptamer in the mRNA leader senses thiamine pyrophosphate; ligand binding refolds the expression platform into a terminator/sequestering structure that switches the downstream gene off, while the ligand-free form keeps it on.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Nascent mRNA w/ aptamer"]
    N2{"TPP metabolite present?"}
    N3["TPP binds aptamer"]
    N4["Expression platform refolds"]
    N5["Terminator hairpin forms"]
    N6["Gene expression OFF"]
    N7["Anti-terminator default"]
    N8["Gene expression ON"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 -->|TPP high| N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N2 -->|TPP low| N7
    N7 --> N8

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

nodeDetails.label: Nascent mRNA w/ aptamer
nodeDetails.detail: Leader RNA bearing a TPP aptamer

sources.title: Thiamine derivatives bind messenger RNAs directly to regulate bacterial gene expression
sources.authors: Winkler, W.; Nahvi, A.; Breaker, R. R.

sources.title: Sensing small molecules by nascent RNA: a mechanism to control transcription in bacteria
sources.authors: Mironov, A. S.; Gusarov, I.; Rafikov, R.; Lopez, L. E.; Shatalin, K.; Kreneva, R. A.; Perumov, D. A.; Nudler, E.
